channel.title: Comments for page "A Storm More Foul, A Land Less Fair"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Storm More Foul, A Land Less Fair"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some nice introspection from the protagonist. I don't know what this used to be like, seems this has been revised a bit, but currently it's pretty good. There is some unnecessary repetition around the middle portion of the story, but not enough to be a major issue for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh.

A lot of reflection here, and a lot of exposition, but not a whole lot of payoff. The whole tale trudges along awkwardly from the King's thought on this and that, building up to a moment where we finally see him take action…

But then the tale cuts out.

I'd be fine with the build up if there was a satisfactory payoff, but that is lacking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you are really planning on it, I'd be really excited. I know most of the entries have not ben of your taste so far, but we plan on improving upon them soon. Still, we're thankful for your involvement!

sorry for necroposting btw

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That'd be really cool. Part of me kinda wants to take a crack at this myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: These are fair criticisms, and rereading it with that in mind I see some of the problems with how I've written it. However, since this is a contest entry, I can't make such edits for about three weeks. When the time comes, I'll revisit this and make revisions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Conceptually? Fuck. This is far and away the most interesting submission I've seen for the WoaF Canon.

However, I feel like it suffers from the same problems as other entries into that series, namely, that it's simply over-explaining and trying to cover too much ground.

The paragraphs-long series of asides (speculating on their diet, hypothetical about other worlds, two paragraphs of background detailing the fate of the rest of the world) seem clunky and really take away from the intimacy of the scene. I don't think it'd be terrible to include some of these details, but it feels like it's just a bunch of fluff thrown in in no particular order - it's doesn't feel like it flows seamlessly from one idea to the next.

I wanted to keep reading this, and I will most likely come back to it, but it's not captivatingly written, when it very much could be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reminds me of the story about Constantine XI Paleologous, last Emperor of Constantinople. When the Turkish army breached the city walls, he allegedly exclaimed, "The city fallen, and I am still alive?!", removed his imperial regalia, and rushed out with a sword to die alongside his people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a tale set within SCP-3534-2, taking place during the MH-Class "Large-Scale Aggressor" Overrun scenario that ended in everyone dying. In particular, this is the story of the second attack on Hy-Brasil.

For reference, the spear and the sword are two of the Four Treasures of the Tuatha De Dannan, an established part of Irish mythology.

Thanks to FloppyPhoenix, Cyantreuse and multizig for looking this over, as well as the rest of my Doomcon team: Stallmantic, DrChandra and DrCaroll.
